title: Japanese Plantain Empanada Fritters
description: A unique fusion of Venezuelan empanada dough and Japanese flavors, these crispy fritters are a tasty twist on traditional plantain empanadas.
instructions: 1. Preheat oil in a deep frying pan to 350°F (175°C).
2. Peel and slice the plantain into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
3. In a small bowl, mix together seaweed flakes and sweet soy sauce.
4. Brush the empanada dough with water and cut into desired shapes.
5. Place a plantain slice in the center of each dough shape and brush with seaweed-soy mixture.
6. Fold the dough over the filling and press edges to seal.
7. Carefully place the fritters in the hot oil and fry for 3-4 minutes on each side.
8. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with additional seaweed-soy sauce for dipping.
